commands.repair_reason: Needs maintainer review for stack ordering and the new public SDK/config policy surface; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.; Cleared: Cleared: the current diff changes policy checks, docs, generated SDK metadata, package exports, and config comments, with no current lockfile, workflow, secret, or runtime authorization change. (sha=6b82affe1e566743e354a84422b23300321849c2)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds Policy plugin ingress/channel conformance rules with docs/tests, adds a `plugin-sdk/tool-policy-conformance` export, and carries the stacked tool/sandbox posture commits.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an additive policy feature, not a reported current-main bug. The changed behavior is source-reviewable in the PR head and covered by focused policy doctor tests.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer review for stack ordering and the new public SDK/config policy surface; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: Policy: add ingress channel conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds config-level ingress/channel policy conformance checks for DM scope, DM policy allowlists, open group denial, and mention-required group posture.
- Records ingress evidence only when an ingress policy section is present, so unrelated policy hashes and findings do not drift.
- Documents the offered policy syntax and the deliberately narrow boundary: this checks configured posture, not runtime sender authorization, pairing stores, live membership, or secret management.

Stack note: this PR is stacked after #85482 tool posture and #85572 sandbox posture. The branch has been rebased onto current `origin/main` and preserves those two open policy commits before the ingress/channel commit.

Policy syntax offered:

```jsonc
{
  "ingress": {
    "session": {
      "requireDmScope": "per-channel-peer"
    },
    "channels": {
      "allowDmPolicies": ["pairing", "allowlist", "disabled"],
      "denyOpenGroups": true,
      "requireMentionInGroups": true
    }
  }
}
```

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
  - 2 files passed, 177 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
  - All matched files use the correct format.
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
  - Docs MDX check passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
  - Clean.
- `codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --base HEAD~1`
  - Clean after fixing the accepted provider-specific `rooms`/`teams` ingress finding.
- `pnpm exec tsx /mnt/c/tmp/policy-ingress-cli-proof.ts`
  - Command-handler proof returned the expected ingress findings from a temp config:
    `policy/ingress-dm-scope-unapproved`, `policy/ingress-dm-policy-unapproved`,
    `policy/ingress-open-groups-denied`, and `policy/ingress-group-mention-required`.
  - The emitted evidence included `oc://openclaw.config/channels/msteams/teams/engineering/channels/general/requireMention`.

Known proof gap:

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false` is currently blocked by pre-existing WhatsApp/Baileys type errors unrelated to this policy change.
- Full `pnpm openclaw policy check --help` currently fails before policy code runs because the local build cannot resolve `node_modules/protobufjs/google/protobuf/descriptor.json` via diagnostics-otel. `pnpm install` reports the workspace is already up to date, so I used the policy command handler directly for real config/output proof.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` policy conformance can now report nonconforming configured ingress posture for session DM scope, configured channel DM policies, configured group openness, and configured group mention requirements.

Real environment tested: WSL-native OpenClaw checkout under `/root/src/openclaw-policy-ingress-conformance` on the `policy-ingress-rebased` branch, pushed to `giodl73-repo:policy-ingress-channel-conformance`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: Focused policy doctor tests cover allowed and denied DM scopes, DM policies, disabled channel/account skips, nested groups/guilds/channels/topics, provider-specific `rooms` and `teams` containers, inherited supported group defaults, omitted `unspecified` posture, group-policy disabled suppression, and re-enabled nested group mention requirements.

Observed result after fix: 2 test files passed with 177 tests passing; formatting, docs MDX, diff whitespace, and Codex autoreview also passed. The command-handler proof produced the four expected ingress finding IDs and provider-specific Teams evidence from a temp config.

What was not tested: Live provider ingress, pairing stores, live channel membership, sender authorization, and secret/config value inspection, because this PR intentionally implements config conformance only.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
